reason: The total score of 73 and the personality score of 20/25 are broadly defensible - Robbie Coltrane's performance captured Hagrid's warmth, loyalty, and love of creatures faithfully. However, the low narrative_role (14/25) and motivations (12/25) scores are harder to assess without the justification text, but fan/critic consensus does support significant cuts to Hagrid's narrative agency: his giant recruitment mission was heavily truncated, the Umbridge night attack on his hut was cut entirely, his Half-Blood Prince subplot (dropping Care of Magical Creatures class, the apology scene) was omitted, and his Deathly Hallows support party was cut. These omissions genuinely reduce his narrative role and motivations. The arc score of 11/25 seems slightly harsh - the films do carry his core arc (expelled student redeemed, promoted to teacher, loyal to the end) even if emotional beats like comforting Harry after Sirius's death were cut. Overall 73 is a reasonable mid-range score consistent with the pattern of 'personality well-preserved, narrative depth significantly reduced', though the dimension distribution could be debated.
